summary: Unable to implement without repository context for the Deals & Scenarios screen. Need the target file path(s) and confirmation of the UI framework/utilities in use to apply the minimal flex-row layout aligning the Analyze Deal button with the Address input.
completed_work: Prepared a minimal, responsive layout approach per Technical Architect guidance (flex row with gap, input flex:1, button auto-width, mobile stacking)
Outlined QA considerations (no new deps, avoid one-off overrides, cross-browser baseline alignment)
artifacts_updated: No repository artifacts updated in this stage
decision_or_outcome: Use existing CSS/utilities only; no new dependencies
Wrap Address input and Analyze Deal button in a shared flex row with gap and align-items set to baseline or flex-end
Ensure responsive behavior: inline on desktop/tablet, stacked full-width button on small screens
next_required_action: Provide the exact file path(s)/component name(s) for the Deals & Scenarios screen containing the Address input and the “Analyze Deal” button, and confirm the styling system in use (e.g., Tailwind classes, CSS Modules, Styled Components, or plain CSS with project tokens).
blockers_or_risks: Unknown UI framework/utilities; cannot implement precise classes without confirmation
Target component/file path is unknown; cannot modify code safely
Potential screen-specific overrides could affect alignment; requires local inspection during change
Safari/Firefox baseline alignment quirks need verification after implementation

implementation_plan.goal: Align the Analyze Deal button horizontally with the Address input, preserving responsive behavior and accessibility.
implementation_plan.issue_body: Place the blue “Analyze Deal” button inline with the Address input field on the Deals & Scenarios screen so the layout appears intentional and symmetric. On small screens, stack the button below the input full-width. No behavioral changes.
recommended_first_changes: Locate the Deals & Scenarios component (search for the button label 'Analyze Deal' and the Address field).
Wrap the Address field and button in a shared row container with display:flex and a consistent gap using existing spacing tokens.
Set the input wrapper to flex:1 1 auto with min-width:0; keep the button intrinsic width.
Use align-items:flex-end or baseline to match visual alignment; verify across browsers.
At small breakpoints, stack vertically and make the button width:100%.
notes: Preserve tab order: label -> address input -> analyze button
Remove ad-hoc margins between elements; rely on gap/utility spacing
No new dependencies or global CSS; keep styles local to the component or utilities already in use

handoff.next_required_action: Share the exact file path(s) and confirm the UI framework/utilities used on the Deals & Scenarios screen so implementation can proceed.
